title: Iran partially restores internet after 88-day blackout — Pezeshkian order, NetBlocks confirms, admin court partially halted, traffic ~10%
snippet: NetBlocks confirms partial internet restoration in Iran after 2,093 hours (88 days) of near-total isolation — longest nationwide internet shutdown in modern history. President Pezeshkian ordered restoration but Administrative Court of Justice temporarily halted implementation. Cost $30-40M/day. Traffic at ~10% of pre-blackout levels. Trump sees it as 'sign Iran wants deal.'
tag_reason: 이란 88일 인터넷 차단 해제는 이전 보고서에 없는 완전 신규 사건.
